qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated dashboard bootstrap leaks configured AI provider API keys
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a GET /parse-dashboard-config.json secret disclosure caused by returning config.agent with unredacted apiKey values to authenticated users, while the advisory is a missing CSRF check on POST /apps/:appId/agent. The endpoint, missing guard, and root cause differ.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing CSRF protection on POST /apps/:appId/agent or an equivalent cross-site request path. The only accepted qualifying finding is a separate authenticated secret-disclosure issue in the dashboard bootstrap config response.
evidence: The advisory summary and details define the vulnerable code/root cause as missing CSRF protection on POST /apps/:appId/agent and state that the fix adds CSRF middleware to that endpoint plus a CSRF token in the dashboard page.
The authoritative session manifest at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_5c1b5934/.judge/input_manifest.json lists only one accepted qualifying finding: agent_api_key_exposure.

The accepted finding report at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_5c1b5934/agent_api_key_exposure/vulnerability_report.json describes a different root cause: GET /parse-dashboard-config.json exposes config.agent.models[*].apiKey to authenticated low-privilege users.
